## computer-use
You have a computer-use MCP available (tools named `mcp__computer-use__*`). It lets you take screenshots of the user's desktop and control it with mouse clicks, keyboard input, and scrolling.

**Pick the right tool for the app.** Each tier trades speed/precision against coverage:

1. **Dedicated MCP for the app** — if the task is in an app that has its own MCP (Slack, Gmail, Calendar, Linear, etc.) and that MCP is connected, use it. API-backed tools are fast and precise.
2. **Chrome MCP** (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`) — if the target is a web app and there's no dedicated MCP for it, use the browser tools. DOM-aware, much faster than clicking pixels. If the Chrome extension isn't connected, ask the user to install it rather than falling through to computer use.
3. **Computer use** — for native desktop apps (Maps, Notes, Finder, Photos, System Settings, any third-party native app) and cross-app workflows. Computer use IS the right tool here — don't decline a native-app task just because there's no dedicated MCP for it.

This is about what's available, not error handling — if a dedicated MCP tool errors, debug or report it rather than silently retrying via a slower tier.

**Look before you assert.** If the user asks about app state (what's open, what's connected, what an app can do), take a screenshot and check before answering. Don't answer from memory — the user's setup or app version may differ from what you expect. If you're about to say an app doesn't support an action, that claim should be grounded in what you just saw on screen, not general knowledge. Similarly, `list_granted_applications` or a fresh `screenshot` is cheaper than a wrong assertion about what's running.

**Loading via ToolSearch — load in bulk, not one-by-one:** if computer-use tools are in the deferred list, load them ALL in a single ToolSearch call: `{ query: "computer-use", max_results: 30 }`. The keyword search matches the server-name substring in every tool name, so one query returns the entire toolkit. Don't use `select:` for individual tools — that's one round-trip per tool.

**Access flow:** before any computer-use action you must call `request_access` with the list of applications you need. The user approves each application explicitly, and you may need to call it again mid-task if you discover you need another application.

**Tiered apps:** some apps are granted at a restricted tier based on their category — the tier is displayed in the approval dialog and returned in the `request_access` response:
- **Browsers** (Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Arc, etc.) → tier **"read"**: visible in screenshots, but clicks and typing are blocked. You can read what's already on screen. For navigation, clicking, or form-filling, use the claude-in-chrome MCP (tools named `mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`; load via ToolSearch if deferred).
- **Terminals and IDEs** (Terminal, iTerm, VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) → tier **"click"**: visible and left-clickable, but typing, key presses, right-click, modifier-clicks, and drag-drop are blocked. You can click a Run button or scroll test output, but cannot type into the editor or integrated terminal, cannot right-click (the context menu has Paste), and cannot drag text onto them. For shell commands, use the Bash tool.
- **Everything else** → tier **"full"**: no restrictions.

The tier is enforced by the frontmost-app check: if a tier-"read" app is in front, `left_click` returns an error; if a tier-"click" app is in front, `type` and `right_click` return errors. The error tells you what tier the app has and what to do instead. `open_application` works at any tier — bringing an app forward is a read-level operation.

**Link safety — treat links in emails and messages as suspicious by default.**
- **Never click web links with computer-use tools.** If you encounter a link in a native app (Mail, Messages, a PDF, etc.), do NOT `left_click` it. Open the URL via the claude-in-chrome MCP instead.
- **See the full URL before following any link.** Visible link text can be misleading — hover or inspect to get the real destination.
- **Links from emails, messages, or unknown-sender documents are suspicious by default.** If the destination URL is at all unfamiliar or looks off, ask the user for confirmation before proceeding.
- **Inside the Chrome extension** you can click links with the extension's tools, but the suspicion check still applies — verify unfamiliar URLs with the user.

**Financial actions - do not execute trades or move money.** Budgeting and accounting apps (Quicken, YNAB, QuickBooks, etc.) are granted at full tier so you can categorize transactions, generate reports, and help the user organize their finances. But never execute a trade, place an order, send money, or initiate a transfer on the user's behalf - always ask the user to perform those actions themselves.

1	---
2	name: claymorphism-theme
3	description: "Claymorphism UI design system added to index.html — tokens, intent, and how to tune puffiness"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: be55589f-11df-4cde-a22e-583e94db29c4
8	---
9	
10	In June 2026 the app was reskinned to a **claymorphism** theme based on mockups in `C:\Users\ibrah\OneDrive\Pictures\Screenshots` (Day A.png, Day B.png, Lesson Tracker.png, Assignment Tracker.png, Backup & Restore.png), produced via "Claude Design".
11	
12	**Key intent: deliberately LESS puffy than the mockups.** Ibrahim wanted Day A/B toned down "a bit" and the lesson tracker "significantly less puffy."
13	
14	## Design tokens (in `:root`, index.html)
15	- `--clay-surface: #ffffff` — card background (replaced per-day tint)
16	- `--clay-field-bg: #f6f8fc` — recessed input background
17	- `--clay-border: rgba(60,80,120,0.08)` — faint hairline replacing old 2px solid colored card borders
18	- `--clay-radius: 20px` / `--clay-radius-sm: 12px`
19	- `--clay-shadow-card: 0 4px 14px rgba(90,110,150,0.10)` — Day A/B cards (deliberately toned down)
20	- `--clay-shadow-tracker: 0 12px 28px rgba(80,100,140,0.16), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.7)` — tracker cards: **puffy 3D** (Ibrahim reversed course — he WANTS the mockup's 3D puffiness on trackers, only slightly reduced)
21	- `--clay-shadow-field-inset` — subtle inset for fields/toggle
22	
23	**To make things puffier/flatter:** increase/decrease the blur + alpha on `--clay-shadow-card` / `--clay-shadow-tracker`. These two tokens are the single knob for puffiness. Note: Day A/B = low-puff; trackers = high-puff (opposite of the first pass).
24	
25	## Font
26	App font is **Century Gothic** (system font on Windows). Stack: `'Century Gothic', 'Futura', 'Montserrat', sans-serif`. Replaced all Montserrat refs (CSS + 2 JS inline styles). Google Fonts Montserrat `<link>` kept as fallback.
27	
28	## Footer (exact mockup replica)
29	- Bar: **solid white**, `border-radius: 20px 20px 0 0`, soft upward shadow (`0 -4px 24px rgba(90,110,150,0.10)`). No blur/translucency.
30	- "Course Planner" left: dark navy #2c3340, 700, 19px.
31	- Right buttons: muted gray text (#5b6473), transparent, **no vertical dividers** (`.footer-divider { display:none }`).
32	- **Active** view button: thin light-gray outlined pill (`border 1px #d7dce5`, white bg, dark #2c3340 text) — wired in `switchDay()` toggling `.active` on lessonTrackerBtn/assignmentTrackerBtn/backupBtn. NOTE: removed the redundant `#lessonTrackerBtn{color}` ID rule so `.active` (lower specificity) isn't overridden.
33	- **Save**: green filled primary. **Canvas**: red text (#e72429) + red brand icon (icon kept per Ibrahim's instruction).
34	- **Save & Reset** now show on **tracker views too** (Day A/B + Lesson/Assignment trackers); hidden only on Backup. Driven by `showSaveReset` in `switchDay()`. Reset already had `lessonTracker`/`assignmentTracker` branches (wipes that tracker's data, with confirm); Save always saves both days.
35	- **Pop buttons**: Canvas, Backup, Reset have a tactile neumorphic look — raised pill by default (pop-out), press-in inset on hover (pop-in). `#backupBtn:not(.active)` so the active outline still wins when Backup is the current view.
36	
37	## Footer tracker pill toggle
38	The footer Lesson Tracker / Assignment Tracker buttons are wrapped in `.footer-tracker-toggle` — a green **segmented pill toggle** (same look as the Day A/B header pill, but green and in the footer). Active segment fills green via `.footer-tracker-toggle button.active` (driven by the existing `footerNavMap` `.active` toggling in `switchDay()`). Backup stays a separate button with its own outlined-active style.
39	NOTE: Ibrahim wanted the pill in the **footer, not the header** — a header version was tried and reverted. Do not move it to the header.
40	
41	## Trackers (Lesson & Assignment) — neumorphic
42	Reworked to match the Claude Design neumorphic mockup:
43	- **Donut rings**: `drawPieChart()` was rewritten from a filled red/green pie into a hollow donut ring (gray track + green progress arc, rounded caps, small accent knob at the leading edge, center `%` + `x/y` text). Hi-DPI aware (devicePixelRatio scaling). Paused = gray ring + "Paused".
44	- `.chart-container` is now a raised neumorphic white disc behind the donut.
45	- **Status checkbox** (`.tracker-item-checkbox`): concave inset when empty, green-filled with white check when done.
46	- **Action button** (`.delete-btn`): was a red "Delete" text button → now a soft circular white button with a red trash SVG icon. Its click handler uses `.closest('.delete-btn')` (since it now contains an SVG).
47	- Tracker card titles are dark navy (#2c3340) with the green underline accent (not all-green).
48	- Column header (`.tracker-grid-header`): uppercase, letter-spaced, muted gray.
49	- "View N more" toggle: soft neumorphic pill.
50	- **Animations**: donut rings sweep from 0→target (eased, ~850ms rAF loop in `drawPieChart`, % and count animate up) and the overall progress bar fills from 0, but **only when entering a tracker view** — `renderTracker(type, animate)` / `calculateAndRenderOverallProgress(type, animate)` get `animate=true` only from `switchDay`. Checkbox toggles redraw instantly (donut) / ease in-place (bar). Don't make every renderTracker animate — Ibrahim wants the replay specifically on clicking Lesson/Assignment Tracker.
51	
52	## Expanded card view — NOT themed (reverted)
53	A claymorphism pass on the expanded view (gradient backdrop, neumorphic nav items/buttons, de-capped labels, transparent left panel, plus an iframe "open in new tab" fallback for sites that block embedding) was made and then **fully reverted** at Ibrahim's request — he wants to consult Claude Design first. The expanded view is back to its original flat styling. Do not re-theme it unless he asks. (Note: many links in the expanded view load in an `<iframe>` and silently fail for sites that send X-Frame-Options/CSP, e.g. YouTube, Google, Canvas — a known limitation, currently unaddressed.)
54	
55	## Backup tab — neumorphic
56	`.backup-container` uses the clay surface + tracker shadow. Inner Export/Import sub-cards are soft inset panels with rounded icon badges (blue download / orange upload) and **gradient buttons** (blue export, orange import). Button IDs (`exportJsonBtn`, `importJsonBtn`, `importFile`) preserved — handlers untouched.
57	
58	## What changed
59	- `body` → soft cool gradient backdrop (fixed)
60	- `.main-header` + `.main-footer` → translucent + backdrop blur
61	- `.tab-nav` → Day A/Day B **segmented pill toggle**; active button fills with `--active-color` (blue Day A / orange Day B auto via body class)
62	- `.course-card` + `.tracker-course-card` → white surface, soft hairline border, soft shadow, 20px radius (was 25px + 2px solid colored border)
63	- Shared title underline helper (`.course-card h3::after`, `.tracker-course-card h3::after`) → full-width 2px line at 0.25 opacity (Ibrahim preferred this original subtle version over a short bold accent)
64	- `.task-list textarea, .task-link` → recessed soft fields (10px radius, inset shadow); focus lifts to white + active-color ring
65	- `.task-type-label` ("Lesson:" / "Assignment:" row labels on Day A/B cards): **14px, normal case** (Ibrahim disliked the all-caps and found 11px too small — source text is already "Lesson:"/"Assignment:", the caps were just `text-transform`). General preference: **avoid all-caps; first-letter capitalization only.**
66	
67	Day color accents (title color, underline, checkboxes, focus ring) still come from `--active-color`, which flips blue↔orange↔green by body class (`day-a`/`day-b`/`tracker-green`).
68

**To make things puffier/flatter:** increase/decrease the blur + alpha on `--clay-shadow-card` / `--clay-shadow-tracker`. These two tokens are the single knob for puffiness. Note: Day A/B = low-puff; trackers = high-puff (opposite of the first pass).

## Font
App font is **Century Gothic** (system font on Windows). Stack: `'Century Gothic', 'Futura', 'Montserrat', sans-serif`. Replaced all Montserrat refs (CSS + 2 JS inline styles). Google Fonts Montserrat `<link>` kept as fallback.

## Footer (exact mockup replica)
- Bar: **solid white**, `border-radius: 20px 20px 0 0`, soft upward shadow (`0 -4px 24px rgba(90,110,150,0.10)`). No blur/translucency.
- "Course Planner" left: dark navy #2c3340, 700, 19px.
- Right buttons: muted gray text (#5b6473), transparent, **no vertical dividers** (`.footer-divider { display:none }`).
- **Active** view button: thin light-gray outlined pill (`border 1px #d7dce5`, white bg, dark #2c3340 text) — wired in `switchDay()` toggling `.active` on lessonTrackerBtn/assignmentTrackerBtn/backupBtn. NOTE: removed the redundant `#lessonTrackerBtn{color}` ID rule so `.active` (lower specificity) isn't overridden.
- **Save**: green filled primary. **Canvas**: red text (#e72429) + red brand icon (icon kept per Ibrahim's instruction).
- **Save & Reset** now show on **tracker views too** (Day A/B + Lesson/Assignment trackers); hidden only on Backup. Driven by `showSaveReset` in `switchDay()`. Reset already had `lessonTracker`/`assignmentTracker` branches (wipes that tracker's data, with confirm); Save always saves both days.
- **Pop buttons**: Canvas, Backup, Reset have a tactile neumorphic look — raised pill by default (pop-out), press-in inset on hover (pop-in). `#backupBtn:not(.active)` so the active outline still wins when Backup is the current view.

## Footer tracker pill toggle
The footer Lesson Tracker / Assignment Tracker buttons are wrapped in `.footer-tracker-toggle` — a green **segmented pill toggle** (same look as the Day A/B header pill, but green and in the footer). Active segment fills green via `.footer-tracker-toggle button.active` (driven by the existing `footerNavMap` `.active` toggling in `switchDay()`). Backup stays a separate button with its own outlined-active style.
NOTE: Ibrahim wanted the pill in the **footer, not the header** — a header version was tried and reverted. Do not move it to the header.

## Trackers (Lesson & Assignment) — neumorphic
Reworked to match the Claude Design neumorphic mockup:
- **Donut rings**: `drawPieChart()` was rewritten from a filled red/green pie into a hollow donut ring (gray track + green progress arc, rounded caps, small accent knob at the leading edge, center `%` + `x/y` text). Hi-DPI aware (devicePixelRatio scaling). Paused = gray ring + "Paused".
- `.chart-container` is now a raised neumorphic white disc behind the donut.
- **Status checkbox** (`.tracker-item-checkbox`): concave inset when empty, green-filled with white check when done.
- **Action button** (`.delete-btn`): was a red "Delete" text button → now a soft circular white button with a red trash SVG icon. Its click handler uses `.closest('.delete-btn')` (since it now contains an SVG).
- Tracker card titles are dark navy (#2c3340) with the green underline accent (not all-green).
- Column header (`.tracker-grid-header`): uppercase, letter-spaced, muted gray.
- "View N more" toggle: soft neumorphic pill.
- **Animations**: donut rings sweep from 0→target (eased, ~850ms rAF loop in `drawPieChart`, % and count animate up) and the overall progress bar fills from 0, but **only when entering a tracker view** — `renderTracker(type, animate)` / `calculateAndRenderOverallProgress(type, animate)` get `animate=true` only from `switchDay`. Checkbox toggles redraw instantly (donut) / ease in-place (bar). Don't make every renderTracker animate — Ibrahim wants the replay specifically on clicking Lesson/Assignment Tracker.

## Expanded card view — NOT themed (reverted)
A claymorphism pass on the expanded view (gradient backdrop, neumorphic nav items/buttons, de-capped labels, transparent left panel, plus an iframe "open in new tab" fallback for sites that block embedding) was made and then **fully reverted** at Ibrahim's request — he wants to consult Claude Design first. The expanded view is back to its original flat styling. Do not re-theme it unless he asks. (Note: many links in the expanded view load in an `<iframe>` and silently fail for sites that send X-Frame-Options/CSP, e.g. YouTube, Google, Canvas — a known limitation, currently unaddressed.)

## Backup tab — neumorphic
`.backup-container` uses the clay surface + tracker shadow. Inner Export/Import sub-cards are soft inset panels with rounded icon badges (blue download / orange upload) and **gradient buttons** (blue export, orange import). Button IDs (`exportJsonBtn`, `importJsonBtn`, `importFile`) preserved — handlers untouched.

## What changed
- `body` → soft cool gradient backdrop (fixed)
- `.main-header` + `.main-footer` → translucent + backdrop blur
- `.tab-nav` → Day A/Day B **segmented pill toggle**; active button fills with `--active-color` (blue Day A / orange Day B auto via body class)
- `.course-card` + `.tracker-course-card` → white surface, soft hairline border, soft shadow, 20px radius (was 25px + 2px solid colored border)
- Shared title underline helper (`.course-card h3::after`, `.tracker-course-card h3::after`) → full-width 2px line at 0.25 opacity (Ibrahim preferred this original subtle version over a short bold accent)
- `.task-list textarea, .task-link` → recessed soft fields (10px radius, inset shadow); focus lifts to white + active-color ring
- `.task-type-label` ("Lesson:" / "Assignment:" row labels on Day A/B cards): **14px, normal case** (Ibrahim disliked the all-caps and found 11px too small — source text is already "Lesson:"/"Assignment:", the caps were just `text-transform`). General preference: **avoid all-caps; first-letter capitalization only.**

Day color accents (title color, underline, checkboxes, focus ring) still come from `--active-color`, which flips blue↔orange↔green by body class (`day-a`/`day-b`/`tracker-green`).

In June 2026 the app was reskinned to a **claymorphism** theme based on mockups in `C:\Users\ibrah\OneDrive\Pictures\Screenshots` (Day A.png, Day B.png, Lesson Tracker.png, Assignment Tracker.png, Backup & Restore.png), produced via "Claude Design".

**Key intent: deliberately LESS puffy than the mockups.** Ibrahim wanted Day A/B toned down "a bit" and the lesson tracker "significantly less puffy."

## Design tokens (in `:root`, index.html)
- `--clay-surface: #ffffff` — card background (replaced per-day tint)
- `--clay-field-bg: #f6f8fc` — recessed input background
- `--clay-border: rgba(60,80,120,0.08)` — faint hairline replacing old 2px solid colored card borders
- `--clay-radius: 20px` / `--clay-radius-sm: 12px`
- `--clay-shadow-card: 0 4px 14px rgba(90,110,150,0.10)` — Day A/B cards (deliberately toned down)
- `--clay-shadow-tracker: 0 12px 28px rgba(80,100,140,0.16), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.7)` — tracker cards: **puffy 3D** (Ibrahim reversed course — he WANTS the mockup's 3D puffiness on trackers, only slightly reduced)
- `--clay-shadow-field-inset` — subtle inset for fields/toggle

**To make things puffier/flatter:** increase/decrease the blur + alpha on `--clay-shadow-card` / `--clay-shadow-tracker`. These two tokens are the single knob for puffiness. Note: Day A/B = low-puff; trackers = high-puff (opposite of the first pass).

## Font
App font is **Century Gothic** (system font on Windows). Stack: `'Century Gothic', 'Futura', 'Montserrat', sans-serif`. Replaced all Montserrat refs (CSS + 2 JS inline styles). Google Fonts Montserrat `<link>` kept as fallback.

## Footer (exact mockup replica)
- Bar: **solid white**, `border-radius: 20px 20px 0 0`, soft upward shadow (`0 -4px 24px rgba(90,110,150,0.10)`). No blur/translucency.
- "Course Planner" left: dark navy #2c3340, 700, 19px.
- Right buttons: muted gray text (#5b6473), transparent, **no vertical dividers** (`.footer-divider { display:none }`).
- **Active** view button: thin light-gray outlined pill (`border 1px #d7dce5`, white bg, dark #2c3340 text) — wired in `switchDay()` toggling `.active` on lessonTrackerBtn/assignmentTrackerBtn/backupBtn. NOTE: removed the redundant `#lessonTrackerBtn{color}` ID rule so `.active` (lower specificity) isn't overridden.
- **Save**: green filled primary. **Canvas**: red text (#e72429) + red brand icon (icon kept per Ibrahim's instruction).
- **Save & Reset** now show on **tracker views too** (Day A/B + Lesson/Assignment trackers); hidden only on Backup. Driven by `showSaveReset` in `switchDay()`. Reset already had `lessonTracker`/`assignmentTracker` branches (wipes that tracker's data, with confirm); Save always saves both days.
- **Pop buttons**: Canvas, Backup, Reset have a tactile neumorphic look — raised pill by default (pop-out), press-in inset on hover (pop-in). `#backupBtn:not(.active)` so the active outline still wins when Backup is the current view.

## Footer tracker pill toggle
The footer Lesson Tracker / Assignment Tracker buttons are wrapped in `.footer-tracker-toggle` — a green **segmented pill toggle** (same look as the Day A/B header pill, but green and in the footer). Active segment fills green via `.footer-tracker-toggle button.active` (driven by the existing `footerNavMap` `.active` toggling in `switchDay()`). Backup stays a separate button with its own outlined-active style.
NOTE: Ibrahim wanted the pill in the **footer, not the header** — a header version was tried and reverted. Do not move it to the header.

## Trackers (Lesson & Assignment) — neumorphic
Reworked to match the Claude Design neumorphic mockup:
- **Donut rings**: `drawPieChart()` was rewritten from a filled red/green pie into a hollow donut ring (gray track + green progress arc, rounded caps, small accent knob at the leading edge, center `%` + `x/y` text). Hi-DPI aware (devicePixelRatio scaling). Paused = gray ring + "Paused".
- `.chart-container` is now a raised neumorphic white disc behind the donut.
- **Status checkbox** (`.tracker-item-checkbox`): concave inset when empty, green-filled with white check when done.
- **Action button** (`.delete-btn`): was a red "Delete" text button → now a soft circular white button with a red trash SVG icon. Its click handler uses `.closest('.delete-btn')` (since it now contains an SVG).
- Tracker card titles are dark navy (#2c3340) with the green underline accent (not all-green).
- Column header (`.tracker-grid-header`): uppercase, letter-spaced, muted gray.
- "View N more" toggle: soft neumorphic pill.
- **Animations**: donut rings sweep from 0→target (eased, ~850ms rAF loop in `drawPieChart`, % and count animate up) and the overall progress bar fills from 0, but **only when entering a tracker view** — `renderTracker(type, animate)` / `calculateAndRenderOverallProgress(type, animate)` get `animate=true` only from `switchDay`. Checkbox toggles redraw instantly (donut) / ease in-place (bar). Don't make every renderTracker animate — Ibrahim wants the replay specifically on clicking Lesson/Assignment Tracker.

## Expanded card view — NOT themed (reverted)
A claymorphism pass on the expanded view (gradient backdrop, neumorphic nav items/buttons, de-capped labels, transparent left panel, plus an iframe "open in new tab" fallback for sites that block embedding) was made and then **fully reverted** at Ibrahim's request — he wants to consult Claude Design first. The expanded view is back to its original flat styling. Do not re-theme it unless he asks. (Note: many links in the expanded view load in an `<iframe>` and silently fail for sites that send X-Frame-Options/CSP, e.g. YouTube, Google, Canvas — a known limitation, currently unaddressed.)

## Backup tab — neumorphic
`.backup-container` uses the clay surface + tracker shadow. Inner Export/Import sub-cards are soft inset panels with rounded icon badges (blue download / orange upload) and **gradient buttons** (blue export, orange import). Button IDs (`exportJsonBtn`, `importJsonBtn`, `importFile`) preserved — handlers untouched.

## What changed

- `.main-header` + `.main-footer` → translucent + backdrop blur
- `.tab-nav` → Day A/Day B **segmented pill toggle**; active button fills with `--active-color` (blue Day A / orange Day B auto via body class)

- Shared title underline helper (`.course-card h3::after`, `.tracker-course-card h3::after`) → full-width 2px line at 0.25 opacity (Ibrahim preferred this original subtle version over a short bold accent)

- `.task-type-label` ("Lesson:" / "Assignment:" row labels on Day A/B cards): **14px, normal case** (Ibrahim disliked the all-caps and found 11px too small — source text is already "Lesson:"/"Assignment:", the caps were just `text-transform`). General preference: **avoid all-caps; first-letter capitalization only.**

Day color accents (title color, underline, checkboxes, focus ring) still come from `--active-color`, which flips blue↔orange↔green by body class (`day-a`/`day-b`/`tracker-green`).
